heading: Firearm and bow hunter education and safety program grants
label: 669h–1 Firearm and bow hunter education and safety program grants
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 10 of act , was renumbered section 12 and is classified to .Sept. 2, 1937section 669i of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the enhancement of hunter education programs, hunter and sporting firearm safety programs, and hunter development programs;

children.label: ii
children.text: the enhancement of interstate coordination and development of hunter education and shooting range programs;

children.label: iii
children.text: the enhancement of bow hunter and archery education, safety, and development programs;

children.label: iv
children.text: the enhancement of construction or development of firearm shooting ranges and archery ranges, and the updating of safety features of firearm shooting ranges and archery ranges; and

children.label: v
children.text: the enhancement of hunter recruitment and recreational shooter recruitment; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a State that has used all of the funds apportioned to the State under for the fiscal year in the manner described in , any use authorized by this chapter (including hunter safety programs and the construction, operation, and maintenance of public target ranges).section 669c(c) of this titlesection 669g(b) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on use
children.label: 2 Limitation on use
children.text: Under paragraph (1), a State shall not be required to use more than the amount described in for hunter safety programs and the construction, operation, and maintenance of public target ranges.section 669g(b) of this title

children.heading: Allocation of additional amounts
children.label: 3 Allocation of additional amounts
children.text: Of the amount apportioned to a State for any fiscal year under , the State may elect to allocate not more than 10 percent, to be combined with the amount apportioned to the State under paragraph (1) for that fiscal year, for acquiring land for, expanding, or constructing a public target range.section 669c(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Federal share of the cost of any activity carried out using a grant under this section shall not exceed 75 percent of the total cost of the activity.

children.heading: Public target range construction or expansion
children.label: 2 Public target range construction or expansion
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of acquiring land for, expanding, or constructing a public target range in a State on Federal or non-Federal land pursuant to this section or shall not exceed 90 percent of the cost of the activity.section 669g(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), amounts made available and apportioned for grants under this section shall remain available only for the fiscal year for which the amounts are apportioned.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Amounts provided for acquiring land for, constructing, or expanding a public target range shall remain available for expenditure and obligation during the 5-fiscal-year period beginning on October 1 of the first fiscal year for which the amounts are made available.

children.heading: Reapportionment
children.label: 2 Reapportionment
children.text: At the end of the period of availability under paragraph (1), the Secretary of the Interior shall apportion amounts made available that have not been used to make grants under this section among the States described in subsection (a)(1)(B) for use by those States in accordance with this chapter.
